join_order.notes: Materialize a dense uint32_t array keyed by global adsh code that stores sub.name only for FY 2023 filings; non-qualifying codes keep the empty sentinel so the hot loop does one array lookup instead of a rowid lookup plus fy check.

Pack the stmt='IS' rows into adsh-local contiguous slices with SoA payload arrays for tag, version, and plabel; for each surviving num row, use adsh to fetch the slice and linearly compare the ~20-row average candidate list to find matching (tag, version).

data_structures.dim_filter: flat_array(filtered_name_by_adsh) built from sub_fy_postings + sub.adsh + sub.name
data_structures.fact_join: flat_array(pre_is_offsets_by_adsh) + compact SoA payload arrays pre_is_tag/pre_is_version/pre_is_plabel over stmt='IS' rows

optimization_notes: Root cause: the iter_1 hot path still performs about 10.3M probes into the persisted `pre_adsh_tag_version_groups` hash, whose 33,554,432 24-byte buckets alone are about 768 MiB and far exceed the 88 MiB LLC, making the join latency-bound on random cache misses. Strategy category B/C: keep the successful sub-first order, but replace the cache-exceeding triple-hash probe with a compact `stmt='IS'` payload grouped by `adsh`. Build a dense `filtered_name_by_adsh` array from `sub_fy_postings`, then pack the 1,767,049 `IS` pre rows into contiguous adsh-local slices storing only `(tag, version, plabel)`. This working set is about 21.2 MiB for payload plus less than 1 MiB of dense offset arrays, so the hot loop becomes dense-array lookups plus short sequential scans over about 20.6 `IS` rows per filing on average instead of random probes into an 805 MiB structure.
